primary_sources.title: The Update Framework Specification 1.0.34
relevant_findings: threshold or quorum trust limits the effect of fewer-than-threshold key compromises
signatures from one key must not be counted more than once toward a threshold
freshness and rollback resistance require retained trusted state and validity windows

primary_sources.title: Node.js Crypto API
relevant_findings: Ed25519 verification is available through node:crypto verify with a null algorithm parameter
public keys can be loaded from PEM SubjectPublicKeyInfo material

finding: A divergent two-replica state cannot be safely resolved by choosing the highest sequence. Resolution requires a separately configured authority set, threshold-valid signatures over the exact candidate digest and sequence, freshness checks, unique signer counting, and fail-closed handling of conflicting threshold statements.
design_implication: Add a pure resolver that accepts only threshold-signed, unexpired statements for one of the observed divergent candidates. Reject insufficient signatures, duplicate signer inflation, outside candidates, expired statements, and simultaneous threshold authorization of different candidates.
uncertainty: The test harness uses generated in-process Ed25519 keys and does not prove independent administration, hardware protection, remote availability, or secure root-policy distribution.
falsification_route: Provide a statement that the resolver accepts without threshold-valid unique signatures, after expiry, for a non-candidate state, or while another threshold-valid statement authorizes a different candidate.
trust_limit: Compromise of threshold keys, stale or malicious root policy, or collusion among threshold authorities can authorize a false state. Cryptographic authorization does not prove factual truth.
